(2,0).title: CampEntry
(2,0).description: The camp's east edge is marked by a line of weathered stakes driven into the gravel at intervals — a boundary worn smooth by seasons of river wind. The ground inside is packed harder than the bank beyond it, darkened by years of foot traffic. Lean-tos of oiled canvas stand to the west, angled against the prevailing wind. The smell of woodsmoke and dried meat is warm and immediate.

props.name: Camp Boundary
props.description: A line of weathered stakes marks the camp's eastern edge. Beyond them, the bank continues east toward the descent road.
props.idle_message: The camp boundary leads back out to the descent road.
props.discovery_message: the camp boundary leading back east!
props.announce: The camp's eastern boundary opens onto the descent road.

(1,0).title: RiversEdge
(1,0).description: The camp's western edge meets the river approach — packed gravel dropping to the waterline in a shallow ramp worn by use. The river is wide here, grey-green, moving with a quiet authority. A flat-bottomed craft is pulled up on the gravel and tied to a stake driven into the bank. The far shore is visible: lower ground, darker soil.

(0,0).title: WaterStep
(0,0).description: A short muddy track ends at a platform of weathered planks spiked into the gravel bank. Drag marks in the mud show where the craft is regularly hauled up and launched. The river is close here — cold water and silt smell, the constant low sound of current. The far bank is a dark line through the grey.

props.name: Ferry Landing
props.description: A flat-bottomed craft sits on the gravel, rope-tied to the bank stake. It could carry two people and gear across the river.
props.idle_message: The ferry craft is tied at the landing, ready for the crossing west.
props.announce: The ferry craft is tied at the landing here.

(1,1).title: FireRing
(1,1).description: A fire ring at the camp's centre, its stones black with years of smoke. The ring is well-maintained — ash cleared, wood stacked to one side in a manner that suggests habit rather than recent effort. A blackened iron pot hangs from a simple tripod above a bed of coals. The smell here is woodsmoke, dried meat, something with herbs.

(2,1).title: CampFarEdge
(2,1).description: The camp's southeast corner: a low windbreak of stacked stones marking the boundary, and beyond it the open bank continuing east. A cord strung between two stakes carries several small carved shapes — worn smooth, turning slowly in the river wind. The ground here is undisturbed gravel, untouched by the camp's foot traffic.
